note_text:
Patient: 67M presenting with moderate rheumatoid arthritis.
Conservative management including dietary and lifestyle modification, NSAIDs was attempted over approximately 17 weeks without adequate improvement.
Imaging studies confirm findings consistent with rheumatoid arthritis.
Referring rheumatology physician requests authorization for biologic therapy.

Patient: 36F presenting with severe rheumatoid arthritis.
Conservative management including bracing was attempted over approximately 8 weeks without adequate improvement.
Imaging workup is still pending.
Referring rheumatology physician requests authorization for biologic therapy.

Patient: 41F presenting with rheumatoid arthritis.
Conservative management including bracing was attempted over approximately 12 weeks without adequate improvement.
Imaging workup is still pending.
Referring rheumatology physician requests authorization for biologic therapy.

Patient: 58M presenting with moderate psoriatic arthritis.
Conservative management including NSAIDs, antiarrhythmic medication was attempted over approximately 17 weeks without adequate improvement.
Imaging studies confirm findings consistent with psoriatic arthritis.
Referring rheumatology physician requests authorization for biologic therapy.

Patient: 77M presenting with active psoriatic arthritis.
Conservative management including activity modification was attempted over approximately 5 weeks without adequate improvement.
Imaging studies confirm findings consistent with psoriatic arthritis.
Referring rheumatology physician requests authorization for biologic therapy.

Patient: 43M presenting with psoriatic arthritis.
Conservative management including activity modification, disease-modifying medication was attempted without adequate improvement.
Imaging workup is still pending.
Referring rheumatology physician requests authorization for biologic therapy.

Patient: 66M presenting with active ankylosing spondylitis.
Conservative management including physical therapy, dietary and lifestyle modification was attempted over approximately 15 weeks without adequate improvement.
Imaging studies confirm findings consistent with ankylosing spondylitis.
Referring rheumatology physician requests authorization for biologic therapy.

Patient: 64F presenting with severe ankylosing spondylitis.
Conservative management including physical therapy was attempted over approximately 5 weeks without adequate improvement.
Imaging studies confirm findings consistent with ankylosing spondylitis.
Referring rheumatology physician requests authorization for biologic therapy.

Patient: 72F presenting with ankylosing spondylitis.
Conservative management including preventive medication trial was attempted without adequate improvement.
Imaging workup is still pending.
Referring rheumatology physician requests authorization for biologic therapy.

Patient: 60F presenting with severe systemic lupus erythematosus.
Conservative management including disease-modifying medication, physical therapy was attempted over approximately 8 weeks without adequate improvement.
Imaging workup is still pending.
Referring rheumatology physician requests authorization for ivig therapy.

Patient: 68F presenting with severe systemic lupus erythematosus.
No conservative therapy has been documented yet.
Imaging studies confirm findings consistent with systemic lupus erythematosus.
Referring rheumatology physician requests authorization for ivig therapy.

gold.imaging_evidence: findings consistent with systemic lupus erythematosus

note_text:
Patient: 67F presenting with refractory systemic lupus erythematosus.
Conservative management including dietary and lifestyle modification, bracing was attempted over approximately 14 weeks without adequate improvement.
Imaging workup is still pending.
Referring rheumatology physician requests authorization for ivig therapy.
